LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT POSITIVE RESOURCE CENTER'S MOTION TO COMPEL INDEPENDENT MEDICAL EXAMINATION OF PLAINTIFF IS GRANTED. PLAINTIFF IS TO UNDERGO A MEDICAL EXAMINATION AT DR'S OFFICE LOCATED AT 50 CALIFORNIA STREET, STE. 1500, SAN FRANCISCO, CA 94111. THE EXAMINATION MAY LAST UP TO 8 HOURS AS DETERMINED BY THE DOCTOR. THE EXAMINATION WILL CONSIST OF AN OPEN ENDED INTERVIEW IN WHICH THE PLAINTIFF'S MEDICAL AND SUCH HISTORY WILL BE GATHERED AND MENTAL STATUS ASSESSED. THE EXAMINATION WILL INCLUDE INQUIRIES INTO PLAINTIFF'S CURRENT COMPLAINTS, HISTORY AND CHARACTER OF PREVIOUSL PSYCHOLOGICAL/PSYCHIATRIC COMPLAINTS AND DIAGNOSIS, MEDICAL HISTORY, WORK HISTORY, FAMILY MEDICAL AND STRICT HISTORY, SOCIAL AND DEVELOPMENTAL HISTORY. NO ATTORNEYS/OTHER PARTIES TO BE PRESENT DURING THE EXAMINATION. THE DOCTOR MAY ELECTRONICALLY RECORD EXAMINATION. PLAINTIFF MAY ALSO DO SO. A FOLLOW UP EXAMINATION WILL OCCUR, IF PLAINTIFF WISHES. THE EXAMINATION CANNOT BE A PHYSICAL OR WRITTEN TEST. FURTHER, THE EXAMINATION CANNOT BE PAINFUL, OR CONDUCTED USING INVASIVE PROCEDURES. THIS ORDER PERTAINS ONLY TO DISCOVERY AND IS WITHOUT PREJUDICE TO ANY MOTION OR ARGUMENT THAT EITHER SIDE MAY MAKE RE: WHAT OPINIONS OR EVIDENCE MY BE PERMISSABLE AT TRIAL. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: MARLA J. MILLER, CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, REPORTER: LAURA MARTINEZ, CSR# 11332, 415.250.2926.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Independent Medical Examiniation Of Pltf
Off Calendar Mar-26-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: larry organ, esq., 415.453.4740, for plaintiff jane gelfand.
sara church reese, esq., 415.986.5900, for defendant positive resource center.

RULING: matter argued. at conclusion of argument, the court vacates its tentative ruling and orders the following: defendant positive resource center's motion to compel independent medical examination of plaintiff is granted. plaintiff is to undergoe a medical examination at dr's office located at 50 california street, ste. 1500 san francisco, ca 94111. the examination may last up to 8 hours as determined by the doctor. the examination will consist of an open ended interview in which the plaintiff's medical and such history will be gathered and mental status assessed. The examination will include inquiries into plaintiff's current complaints, history and character of previousl psychological/psychiatric complaints and diagnosis, medical history, work history, family medical and strict history, social and developmental history. no attorneys/other parties to be present during the examination. the doctor may electronically record examination. Plaintiff may also do so. A follow up examination will occur, if plaintiff wishes. The examination cannot be a physical or written test. Further, the examination cannot be painful, or conducted using invasive procedures. This order pertains only to discovery and is without prejudice to any motion or argument that either side may make re: what opinions or evidence my be permissable at trial. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Laura Martinez, CSR# 11332, 415.250.2926.
